name: Zomato Search V3 — Wildcard-Flexible Search Engine
scenario: Upgrade Zomato Search V2 (Module 8.3) with wildcard-based APIs that accept any compatible collection type. The search engine now accepts `Collection<? extends Restaurant>` so callers can pass Set<RestaurantChain>, List<LocalRestaurant>, or any other collection of Restaurant subtypes — all through the same methods.
description: Refactor the restaurant search engine to use wildcards at all API boundaries. Methods that read from collections use `? extends`, methods that accumulate results use `? super`. Demonstrates the practical PECS rule in a real search domain.

core.description: `Restaurant` base class + `ChainRestaurant extends Restaurant` (has chainId) + `LocalRestaurant extends Restaurant` (has ownerName). Both have the same base fields: name, cuisine, city, rating, pricePerPerson.

`RestaurantSearch` with wildcard-based methods:
- `filterByCuisine(Collection<? extends Restaurant>, String)` — returns `List<Restaurant>`
- `filterByRating(Collection<? extends Restaurant>, double minRating)` — returns `List<Restaurant>`
- `totalRestaurants(Collection<?>)` — unbounded: count any collection
- `addAll(List<? super Restaurant>, Collection<? extends Restaurant>)` — PECS merge
`Statistics<T extends Number & Comparable<T>>` (from Topic 9.3) reused here — compute price stats for `Collection<? extends Restaurant>` using `mapToDouble` on `restaurant.getPricePerPerson()`.
main() demonstrates: mixed collection of Chain and Local restaurants, filter by cuisine/rating, PECS merge two separate lists into one, price statistics.
All filter methods return `List<Restaurant>` so callers always get a typed result.
No casts. Wildcards used correctly at each boundary.
A static `printSummary(Collection<?> restaurants)` that works for any collection of restaurants.

bonus.description: Add `<T extends Restaurant> Optional<T> findBestRated(Collection<? extends T> restaurants)` — uses wildcard to find max-rated from any restaurant subtype collection, preserving subtype T.
Add `groupByCuisine(Collection<? extends Restaurant>)` returning `Map<String, List<Restaurant>>` — groups by cuisine using wildcards.
Add a method `topN(Collection<? extends Restaurant>, int n, Comparator<? super Restaurant>)` — returns top N by any comparator, demonstrating `? super T` on the comparator parameter.

data_to_use:
Chain restaurants (ChainRestaurant):
- Domino's, Pizza, Hyderabad, 4.2, ₹350
- McDonald's, Burgers, Mumbai, 4.0, ₹280
- KFC, Chicken, Hyderabad, 4.3, ₹320

Local restaurants (LocalRestaurant):
- Spice Garden, Indian, Hyderabad, 4.5, ₹300
- Curry Leaf, Indian, Mumbai, 4.7, ₹400
- Noodle House, Chinese, Hyderabad, 4.1, ₹250
example_output:
=== Total count (unbounded) ===
All restaurants: 6
Chain only: 3

=== Filter by cuisine (? extends Restaurant) ===
Indian: [Spice Garden, Curry Leaf]

=== Filter by rating ≥ 4.3 ===
Top rated: [KFC, Spice Garden, Curry Leaf]

=== PECS merge all into one list ===
Merged (6): [Domino's, McDonald's, KFC, Spice Garden, Curry Leaf, Noodle House]

=== Price stats ===
Avg price: ₹316.67 | Min: ₹250.0 | Max: ₹400.0

criteria: Price stats on ? extends collection (15 pts)
Correct wildcard at each boundary (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Core design: filterByCuisine reads from `Collection<? extends Restaurant>` — each element is a Restaurant (or subtype), readable as Restaurant. addAll writes to `List<? super Restaurant>` — adds Restaurants. totalRestaurants takes `Collection<?>` — only needs size(). Bonus: findBestRated signature preserves T to avoid widening to Restaurant.
submission_instructions: Save as ZomatoV3.java. Comment: '// Wildcard-flexible search engine — accepts any Restaurant subtype collection'. Push to GitHub.
tips_for_success: filterByCuisine: `Collection<? extends Restaurant> restaurants` — iterate, each is Restaurant, check cuisine
addAll: `List<? super Restaurant> dest, Collection<? extends Restaurant> src` — for each r in src, dest.add(r)
priceStats: loop through `Collection<? extends Restaurant>`, call .getPricePerPerson() on each (Restaurant method)
For bonus findBestRated: `<T extends Restaurant> Optional<T> findBestRated(Collection<? extends T>)` — T preserved
concepts_reinforced: Unbounded ? for size and structure operations
